_readme: Claims manifest. Every quantitative claim this project makes in user-facing
text, paired with the measurement that regenerates it from the tree.
Checked by scripts/claims_check.py, which is wired into CI and pre-push.
Adding a claim to any surface without adding a row here is the failure this
file exists to prevent; the checker cannot detect a claim it was never told
about, so a row is part of writing the claim, not a follow-up to it.
kind:
exact  -- claimed value must equal the measured value
floor  -- claimed value must be <= the measured value. LIMITATION: a floor
claim can go silently false in the other direction. A hardcoded
'coverage 86%+' badge stays green when coverage falls to 80%,
because 86 <= 80 is the only thing that would fail and it does
not. Floor rows detect overstatement, never decay. The only real
fix is a dynamic badge; see FOLLOWUPS.md item 26.
surface_kind:
repo      -- file in this repository. 'locator' must appear in it verbatim,
so a reworded claim fails loudly instead of going unchecked.
external  -- published outside this repository and unreachable from CI.
Only the recorded value is compared against measurement, so
updating the row and editing the surface are one act.

claims.locator: Running all 7 analyzers over captured queries
CI enforces a minimum of **85% coverage**
CI enforces a minimum of 85% coverage

deferred.reason: The Django Forum announcement post is not reachable from CI and cannot be edited from the repository. It has said 434 since 2026-03-16. Failing the gate on it would make every build red for a surface no commit can fix.
deferred.action: Edit post 117670 during the S14 external-surfaces pass, then delete this deferral. The gate fails if the row is still marked deferred once it agrees with the tree, so the deferral cannot outlive the fix.

_note: Rules that reject a shape of claim rather than check a number.
Applied to the same explicit file list the docs truth sweep gates.

build_time.reason: Build durations are not properties of the tree. The same `mkdocs build --strict` produced 4.14s and 7.89s on different runs; quoting either as a fact makes the artifact wrong on the next machine.

dated_status.reason: A dated assertion about something's CURRENT status rots without anyone touching the file. comparison.md said fetch modes were 'unreleased as of 2026-07-14', which becomes false when Django 6.1 ships. Dated PROVENANCE claims are the opposite -- 'comparisons current as of <date>' records when a comparison was made and stays true permanently -- so they are exempt.

_gated_files_note: Explicit list, deliberately not a repo-root glob: SPEC.md and CLAUDE.md are
gitignored by decision (D-B2), and a glob would gate content that is not in
the repository at all. CHANGELOG.md is excluded from the prose rules because
its historical entries record what was true at the time of each release --
the same permanence argument that exempts dated-provenance claims.
